Metadata.title: João Gaspar | Innovation Director & BIM Specialist
Metadata.description: Innovation Director at totalCAD, BIM Professor, and technical book author. Over 26 years of experience in architecture, engineering, and technology.

Hero.name: João Gaspar
Hero.subtitle: Architect and Urban Planner
Hero.role1: Professor, researcher, and developer of BIM-related applications
Hero.role3: Director at the Brazilian BIM Company - EBBIM
Hero.about1_3: , the Brazilian Committee responsible for developing and adopting BIM standards in Brazil. Currently, he is finishing his PhD at
Hero.about1_5: , the Faculty of Architecture, Urbanism, and Design of the University of São Paulo, researching BIM project contracting for Social Housing.
Hero.about2_7: , he leads digital transformation initiatives in the AECO sector. He is a postgraduate BIM professor at
Hero.about3_5: on ResearchGate, and author of several technical books on SketchUp Pro, Revit, ArchiCAD, among others, published by
Hero.about3_7: , he has dedicated his career to BIM education and dissemination for over 26 years.

Contact.title: Contact
Contact.subtitle: Get in touch regarding innovation and BIM
Contact.description: João Gaspar is always available to discuss new projects, collaboration opportunities, academic research, or consulting in BIM and technology for civil construction and infrastructure.
Contact.card_desc: Schedule a consulting session on BIM processes, management, and coordination.

items.title: PhD Candidate in BIM
items.company: FAU USP - Faculty of Architecture, Urbanism, and Design of the University of São Paulo
items.description: Research project: Proposition of a tool to compare the performance of drawing-based or BIM-based social housing project deliveries in public procurements in Brazil

items.title: Innovation Director (CINO)
items.description: Leads the company's innovation and digital transformation strategy, developing technological solutions for the AECO sector.

items.title: Operations Director
items.description: Founded and directs the leading BIM company in Brazil, originally established in 2000 as a school. EBBIM offers courses on BIM model analysis and approval, IFC, Trimble Connect, SketchUp Pro for BIM, Revit, among others. Currently, besides courses, EBBIM provides specialized consulting, implementation in public and private companies, professional certification, and BIM software development.

items.title: ABNT CEE-134 Coordinator
items.company: ABNT - Brazilian Association of Technical Standards
items.description: Coordinates the Brazilian Committee responsible for developing and adopting BIM standards for Brazil, including ABNT NBR 15965 and ABNT NBR 19650.

items.title: Postgraduate BIM Professor
items.company: Universidade Presbiteriana Mackenzie, FIA Business School, SENAI-ISEP
items.description: Teaches BIM courses in postgraduate programs, including BIM Methodologies, Data Management, and Professional Management in the Digital Age.

Tese.heading: Proposal of a Tool for Comparing ECD and BIM Project Delivery Performance in Social Housing
Tese.desc: Methodological instrument to evaluate and compare Social Housing (HIS) projects procured by Drawing-Centric Delivery (ECD) or BIM in public procurements in Brazil.
Tese.banca_desc: Slides presented at the public doctoral defense at the Faculty of Architecture and Urbanism of USP.
Tese.guia_desc: Documentation and usage instructions for the methodological instruments developed for the thesis.
Tese.igep_desc: Design Effort Index (IGEP) — Part 1 instrument for selecting and pairing the study objects.
Tese.ecd_desc: Part 2 instrument for evaluating and comparing delivery performance indicators between ECD and BIM projects.

items.title: Efficiency and Accuracy in BIM Quantity Takeoff Application to Flashings and Gutters through IFC-Based Computational Methods
items.description: Analysis of BIM application in flashing and gutter quantity takeoff, focusing on budget data reliability through IFC-based computational methods.

items.title: BIM Element Coding for Budgeting in Housing Projects
items.description: Methodology to automate quantity takeoff in Social Housing projects within public procurements.

items.title: Prioritization Method for Mapping Classification Systems for Cost Estimates
items.description: Mapping between IFC and secondary classification systems for faster and more reliable budget studies in BIM.

items.title: BIM in public administration: Topics for project procurement
items.description: Chapter of the ptBIM 2024 Congress proceedings book, on BIM implementation in the Brazilian Public Administration.

items.title: Automated IFC analysis application for budget studies
items.description: Development of an application for auditing IFC models in the Água Espraiada Consortium Urban Operation for the Housing Secretariat of São Paulo.

items.title: Algorithm for calculating areas of complex geometries in IFC models
items.description: Research on processes of quantity takeoff and verification from BIM models, focusing on less recurrent geometries.

items.title: The meaning attributed to BIM over time
items.description: Master's thesis at UNICAMP. Research on the evolution of the meaning of Building Information Modeling through Systematic Literature Review and Scientometric Analysis.

items.title: Integrated and Collaborative Architectural Design: 10 Years of Experience Teaching BIM
items.description: Proceedings of the 35th CIB W78 2018 Conference. Evaluation of the collaborative process mediated by BIM over ten years of teaching.

books.title: ArchiCAD passo a passo - Volume I

books.title: ArchiCAD passo a passo - Volume II
books.description: Second part of the ArchiCAD publication, covering advanced topics in modeling, documentation, and BIM project management.

books.title: SketchUp para design de móveis
books.description: Practical SketchUp guide focused on furniture design, with exercises and 3D modeling techniques applied to furniture.

books.title: SketchUp LayOut passo a passo
books.description: SketchUp LayOut guide, for those who want to learn how to create technical drawing sheets from SketchUp Pro models.

books.title: Vectorworks passo a passo
books.description: Book on the Vectorworks software for architecture, interior design, and landscaping projects.

Igep.meta_desc: IGEP Calculator — Design Effort Grade Indicator for Social Housing projects.
Igep.heading: Design Effort Grade Indicator (IGEP) Calculator
Igep.intro: Select the 11 project characteristics to calculate the IGEP
Igep.formula_body: IGEP = Π (1 + fᵢ) for all 11 criteria. A neutral project (all factors zero) = 1.000.
Igep.c1_desc: Height difference between the highest and lowest point of the terrain.
Igep.c3_desc: Floor plan shape of the typical floor slab.
Igep.c4_desc: Spatial configuration of the typical floor plan, from 1 (simple) to 8 (complex).
Igep.c6_desc: Position of the staircase/elevator shaft relative to the block.
Igep.c7_desc: From most linear to most irregular
Igep.c8_desc: Number of distinct unit types on the typical floor.
Igep.c10_desc: Number and diversity of staircase shafts in the block.

Guia.meta_desc: Appendix A — Methodological Guide for the Tool for Comparing ECD and BIM Project Delivery Performance in Social Housing. Doctoral Thesis FAUUSP 2026.
Guia.heading: Tool Guide
Guia.cover_question: Regarding social housing (HIS) projects, how can one determine when BIM-centred procurement is more suitable than drawing-centred procurement?
Guia.cover_title: Tool for Comparing ECD and BIM Project Delivery Performance
Guia.intro_p1: The Brazilian Government has made considerable efforts and invested resources to promote BIM since 2009. This movement in the public sector has been driven by the potential benefits that BIM can bring to project procurement, construction monitoring, and asset management.
Guia.intro_p2: In 2021, the incentive to use BIM gained new momentum with its inclusion in the Public Procurement Act 14,133/2021, which replaced previous legislation on the subject, as determined by paragraph 3 of Art. 19:
Guia.intro_law: In procurements for engineering and architecture works and services, whenever appropriate to the object of the procurement, Building Information Modelling (BIM) or similar and more advanced integrated technologies and processes shall preferably be adopted.
Guia.intro_law_cite: Brazil. Act No. 14,133, of 1 April 2021, Art. 19, §3 — our emphasis
Guia.intro_p3: The wording of paragraph 3 of Art. 19 requires that BIM shall be used in procurements whenever appropriate to their object.
Guia.intro_p4: The question facing the public official responsible for structuring a social housing (HIS) project procurement, regarding BIM, is therefore:
Guia.intro_question: Regarding HIS projects, how can one tell when BIM-centred procurement is more appropriate than drawing-centred procurement?
Guia.intro_p4b: To answer this question, the public official must consider several factors. Some of the points to be considered are presented below:
Guia.consid_a: Would BIM model delivery lead to a better understanding of what was designed compared to drawing-centred delivery?
Guia.consid_b: Would project delivery using BIM models be completed faster than drawing-centred delivery?
Guia.consid_c: Would projects delivered using BIM models provide better quality information for construction cost estimation compared to drawing-centred projects?
Guia.intro_p5: All of the above points are very important and must be considered by public officials when deciding to require BIM in the procurement documentation.
Guia.intro_p6: The tool presented in this Guide addresses the topic discussed in item (b) above. It allows anyone to compare the delivery performance of social housing (HIS) projects based on Drawing-Centred Delivery (ECD) against projects with BIM model-centred delivery.
Guia.defs_p1: For clarity throughout the text, from this point on we will use the term "ECD Project" to refer to a project whose output is organised around a set of technical drawing sheets. The term "BIM Project" will be used to refer to a project whose main output is the issuance of BIM Models, from which derived technical documents — such as technical drawing sheets — are produced.
Guia.ecd_desc: A project whose output is organised around a set of technical drawing sheets.
Guia.bim_desc: A project whose main output is the issuance of BIM Models, from which derived technical documents — such as technical drawing sheets — are produced.
Guia.purpose_text: This tool is used to compare delivery performance between at least one "ECD Project" and one "BIM Project" in social housing, with the aim of identifying which was delivered faster to a given public entity.
Guia.sec_req: Requirements for selecting projects to be compared
Guia.req_intro: The projects to be compared must be as similar as possible regarding:
Guia.req_item_b: The characteristics of the procurement, from the standpoint of legislation and scope;
Guia.req_item_c: The teams of contracting parties and contractors;
Guia.sec_info: What project information is needed to use the tool?
Guia.info_p1: To use the tool presented in this guide, you will need access to various types of information from a reasonable sample of ECD and BIM projects, so that you can select the most similar ones according to the requirements above and carry out the comparisons.
Guia.info_p2: From the broader perspective of procurement characteristics, the following information is required about the ECD and BIM projects you intend to analyse for selection:
Guia.info_grp1_li1: Procurement data: applicable legislation, the procurement scope (basic or executive design?), and the project start dates.
Guia.info_grp1_li2: Information about the design teams, project management analysts (if applicable), and public supervision staff, to assess the degree of similarity between these actors across the ECD and BIM projects in the sample.
Guia.info_grp1_li3: The design effort grade required to develop each ECD and BIM project to be evaluated. The "design effort grade" is an indicator that numerically summarises the effort needed to develop a given project, based on its programmatic and morphological characteristics defined during the feasibility phase and massing plan, before the decision to use BIM.
Guia.info_grp2_label: About the deliveries of each selected project
Guia.info_grp2_intro: These are the delivery-related pieces of information for each selected ECD and BIM Project that you will need to gather:
Guia.info_grp2_li1: Delivery dates of all project documents to the project manager or supervisor.
Guia.info_grp2_li2: File path lists of documents on the designers' computers. The more correctly structured the folders and subfolders organising a procurement, and the more logically named the documents, the easier and more reliable data extraction will be.
Guia.info_grp2_li3: Information on the number of delivery packages (stages related to validation of design services and payment releases) structuring the procurement.
Guia.info_grp2_li4: Information on the number of delivery versions (intermediate stages, between packages, submitted to contract supervision for review, without payment release).
Guia.info_grp2_li5: Information on the number of revised documents and their revision numbers.
Guia.info_grp2_li6: Information on the contracted disciplines, which can be extracted from file naming conventions.
Guia.info_grp2_li7: Information on the types of documents to be analysed and the file formats to be delivered.
Guia.sec_howto: I have the information. How do I start?
Guia.howto_text: To use the ECD-BIM tool, simply
Guia.sec_support: I am having difficulties. What can I do?
Guia.support_p1: If you are having difficulty using the tool, please write to the author: <email>contato@joaogaspar.com</email>
Guia.supp1_desc: Additional information and details on completing and using the tool worksheets.
Guia.supp2_desc: Direct questions to the author of the research.
Guia.footer_text: João Gaspar · Doctoral Thesis · FAUUSP 2026 · Appendix A — Guide: "When to procure social housing projects with drawing-centred or BIM model-centred delivery?"
